statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simply the complex fraction \frac{{\frac{7}{b-1}-5}}{{\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-10}}}, we multiple the numerator and denominator with the common denominator of {\frac{7}{b-1}-5} and {\frac{1}{b-1}+\frac{1}{b-10}}, which is {\left(b-1\right)\mathopen{}\left(b-10\right)}.
Since b-1 and b-10 were canceled, b \ne 1 and b \ne 10 are in the lost domain condition.
